---
title: "fe_model_female"
author: '18004789'
date: "2025-08-25"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(rstudioapi)
library(rstan)
library(shinystan)
library(ggplot2)
library(bayesplot)
library(gridExtra)
library(MASS)
```

```{r}
X100M_female <- read_excel("100m/100m/100M_female.xls")
X100M_female$Gender <- "F"
# Add a new column 'Year' by extracting the year from 'Date'
X100M_female$Year <- as.numeric(format(X100M_female$Date, "%Y"))

sb_df_female <- X100M_female %>%
  group_by(original_ID, Year) %>%
  # Only keep if there are at least 3 performances in that year
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%
  slice(which.min(Result)) %>%
  ungroup()
sb_df_female$AgeInt <- sb_df_female$Year - as.numeric(format(sb_df_female$DOB, "%Y"))

trim_sb_female <- sb_df_female %>%
  group_by(AgeInt) %>%
  filter(AgeInt >= 17, AgeInt <= 30) %>% # now consider only ages 17 to 30
  ungroup()

# cutting down size of data
# Include athletes with at least 5 years of SBs
trim_sb_female <- trim_sb_female %>%
  group_by(original_ID) %>%
  filter(n() >= 5) %>%
  ungroup()

# Restrict to elite athletes

elite_female_athletes <- trim_sb_female %>%
  group_by(original_ID) %>%
  summarise(PB = min(Result, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  filter(PB < 11.6)

# Keep only SBs for elite athletes
trim_sb_female <- trim_sb_female %>%
  semi_join(elite_female_athletes, by = "original_ID")
```

```{r}
# Create a list of observed ages for each athlete
trim_female_observed_ages <-trim_sb_female %>%
  group_by(original_ID, Competitor) %>%
  summarise(M_i = list(AgeInt), count = n(), y_i = list(Result)) %>%
  ungroup()

trim_female_observed_ages$all_ages <- list(sort(unique(trim_sb_female$AgeInt)))

# getting indicies of observed ages in relation to the vector of all unique ages
trim_female_observed_ages$age_indices <- lapply(trim_female_observed_ages$M_i, function(x) {
  match(x, trim_female_observed_ages$all_ages[[1]])
})
```

```{r}
J <- length(unique(trim_sb_female$AgeInt))
N <- nrow(trim_female_observed_ages)
max_obs_female <- max(trim_female_observed_ages$count)

age_idx_female <- matrix(1, nrow = N, ncol = max_obs_female)
for (i in 1:N) {
  inds <- trim_female_observed_ages$age_indices[[i]]
  len_i <- length(inds)
  age_idx_female[i, 1:len_i] <- inds
}

y_matrix_female <- matrix(0, nrow = N, ncol = max_obs_female)
for(i in 1:N) {
  obs_values <- trim_female_observed_ages$y_i[[i]]
  n_obs <- length(obs_values)
  y_matrix_female[i, 1:n_obs] <- obs_values
}
```

```{r}
female_stan_data <- list(
  N = N,                # number of athletes
  J = J,                # number of unique ages
  max_obs = max_obs_female,    # maximum observations per subject
  obs_count = trim_female_observed_ages$count,  # vector of observation counts per subject minus 1
  age_idx = age_idx_female,    # matrix of precomputed age indices per subject w/o final obs
  y = y_matrix_female,         # padded y matrix with athletes' observed results w/o final obs
  unique_ages = trim_female_observed_ages$all_ages[[1]],  # vector of unique ages 
  center_age = 23.5
)

simple_full <- stan_model('simple_full.stan')
```


```{r}
init_fun <- function() {
  list(
    sigma = rep(0.1, female_stan_data$J),
    Omega = diag(1, female_stan_data$J),
    beta = as.vector(fixed_female_model$coefficients)
  )
}

fe_fit_female_3000  <- sampling(simple_full, data = female_stan_data, init = init_fun, iter = 3000, chains = 1)

# saveRDS(fe_fit_female_3000, "fe_fit_female_3000.rds")
```

```{r}
saveRDS(fe_fit_female_3000, "fe_fit_female_3000.rds")
```


```{r}
unique_ages = trim_female_observed_ages$all_ages[[1]]
obs_count = trim_female_observed_ages$count

posterior_female_fe <- extract(fe_fit_female_3000)

beta_female_samples <- posterior_female_fe$beta
Sigma_female_samples <- posterior_female_fe$Sigma

sigma_j_female_samples <- posterior_female_fe$sigma
apply(sigma_j_female_samples, 2, mean)           
apply(sigma_j_female_samples, 2, quantile, c(0.025, 0.975)) 
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
# conditional predictive distribution
# linear interpolation happens in the plot for gappy cases

n <- 138       # athlete
k <- obs_count[n]     # number of observed ages for this athlete
obs_idx <- age_idx_female[n, 1:k]          # indices of observed ages (e.g., [1, 2, 5, 7])
y_obs <- y_matrix_female[n, 1:k]           # observed performances

# The ages to predict performance for (the "future" ages)
all_age_idx <- 1:J
future_idx <- setdiff(all_age_idx, obs_idx)    # indices of unobserved ages for this athlete

S <- nrow(beta_female_samples)

# Matrix to store posterior predictive samples for each future age
cond_pred_draws <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(future_idx), ncol = S)

for (s in 1:S) {
  mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_female_samples[s, ])
  Sigma <- Sigma_female_samples[s, , ]
  
  # Partition means
  mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
  mu_pred <- mu[future_idx]
  
  # Partition covariance
  Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_12 <- Sigma[future_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
  Sigma_11 <- Sigma[future_idx, future_idx, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Conditional mean and covariance
  mu_cond <- mu_pred + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs)
  Sigma_cond <- Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21)
  
  # Sample from conditional predictive distribution
  cond_pred_draws[, s] <- as.vector(mvrnorm(1, mu_cond, Sigma_cond))
}

# Summarize posterior predictive at each future age
cond_pred_median <- apply(cond_pred_draws, 1, median)
cond_pred_lower  <- apply(cond_pred_draws, 1, quantile, 0.025)
cond_pred_upper  <- apply(cond_pred_draws, 1, quantile, 0.975)

future_ages <- unique_ages[future_idx]

df_pred <- data.frame(
  age = future_ages,
  median = cond_pred_median,
  lower = cond_pred_lower,
  upper = cond_pred_upper
)

df_obs <- data.frame(
  age = unique_ages[obs_idx],
  observed = y_obs
)

ggplot(df_pred, aes(x = age)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_line(aes(y = median), color = "blue", size = 1) +
  geom_point(data = df_obs, aes(x = age, y = observed), color = "black", size = 2) +
  labs(title = paste("Conditional predictive for athlete", n),
       x = "Age", y = "Performance")
```



```{r}
# Out of current athletes, who are running, who is projected to be in 2022 world champs final (1 yr ahead of dataset)

current_female_athlete_ids <- trim_sb_female %>%
  filter((2022 - as.numeric(format(DOB, "%Y"))) <= 30) %>%
  distinct(original_ID) %>%
  pull(original_ID)

current_female_athlete_indicies <- which(trim_female_observed_ages$original_ID %in% current_female_athlete_ids)
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
y_world_final_female <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(current_female_athlete_indicies), ncol = S)

for (ii in seq_along(current_female_athlete_indicies)) {
  n <- current_female_athlete_indicies[ii]
  k <- obs_count[n]
  obs_idx <- age_idx_female[n, 1:k]
  y_obs <- y_matrix_female[n, 1:k]
  
  # current age + 1yr 
  a_plus_1 <- age_idx_female[n, k] + 1
  
  for (s in 1:S) {
    mu <- as.vector(X_poly %*% beta_female_samples[s, ])
    Sigma <- Sigma_female_samples[s, , ]
    
    mu_obs <- mu[obs_idx]
    mu_a1 <- mu[a_plus_1]
    
    Sigma_22 <- Sigma[obs_idx, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_21 <- Sigma[obs_idx, a_plus_1, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_12 <- Sigma[a_plus_1, obs_idx, drop = FALSE]
    Sigma_11 <- Sigma[a_plus_1, a_plus_1, drop = FALSE]
    
    # singular age being predicted, so univariate case
    mu_cond <- as.numeric(mu_a1 + Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, y_obs - mu_obs))
    sigma2_cond <- as.numeric(Sigma_11 - Sigma_12 %*% solve(Sigma_22, Sigma_21))
    
    y_world_final_female[ii, s] <-  rnorm(1, mu_cond, sqrt(sigma2_cond))
  }
}

# For each sample (column), rank the times, rankings[ii, s]: athlete ii's rank (1 = fastest) in sample s
# when rankings are tied, randomly allocate 
rankings <- apply(y_world_final_female, 2, rank, ties.method = "random")

# For each athlete, calculate the proportion of samples where their rank is ≤ 8:
pr_top8 <- rowMeans(rankings <= 8, na.rm = TRUE)

fe_results_world_final_female <- data.frame(
  athlete_idx = current_female_athlete_indicies,
  pr_top8 = pr_top8
)

fe_results_world_final_female$Competitor <- trim_female_observed_ages$Competitor[fe_results_world_final_female$athlete_idx]
```
